{"society_power": 5.5, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": -0.5, "state_change": 1.0, "key_event": "Wartime fiscal-military expansion and repression of radicalism", "reasoning": "Income tax, the census, the Act of Union, and wartime mobilization sharply boosted state extractive, informational, and territorial capacity, while the Combination Acts and Corresponding Societies Act suppressed organized society, leaving its rising industrial/urban base largely latent."}
